1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":      true,
22		"post_run_check_failed": true,
23		"land_conflict":         true,
24	}
25	
26	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
27	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
28	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
29	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
30		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
31	}
32	
33	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
34	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
35	// locking out a provider for too long.
36	//
37	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
38	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
39	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
40	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
41	
42	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
43	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
44		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
45		if probeResult != "" {
46			body += " probe=" + probeResult
47		}
48		if detail != "" {
49			body += "\n" + detail
50		}
51		return body
52	}
53	
54	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
55	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
56	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
57	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
58	
59	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
60	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
61	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
62	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
63	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
64		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
65		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
66		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
67		Status     string  `json:"status"`
68		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
69		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
70	}
71	
72	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
73	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
74	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
75	type EscalationSummary struct {
76		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
77		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
78		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
79		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
80	}
81	
82	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
83	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
84	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
85	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
86	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
87	// SuccessStatus.
88	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
89		out := EscalationSummary{
90			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
91			WinningTier:    winningTier,
92		}
93		if out.WinningTier == "" {
94			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
95		}
96		for _, a := range attempts {
97			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
98			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
99				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
100			}
101		}
102		return out
103	}
104	
105	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
106	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
107	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
108	type BeadEventAppender interface {
109		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
110	}
111	
112	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
113	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
114	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
115	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
116	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
117	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
118		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
119			return nil
120		}
121		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
122		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
123		if err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
127			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
128			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
129			Body:      string(body),
130			Actor:     actor,
131			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
132			CreatedAt: createdAt,
133		})
134	}
135

23	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
24	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
25	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
26	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration
33		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
34		EventSink       io.Writer
35		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
36		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
37		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
65		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
66		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
67		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
68		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
69		Mode         executeloop.Mode
70		IdleInterval time.Duration
71		Once         bool
72		PollInterval time.Duration
73		NoReview     bool
74		LabelFilter  string
75		SessionID    string
76		WorkerID     string
77		ProjectRoot  string
78		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
79		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
80		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
81		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
82		TargetBeadID string
83		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
84		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
85		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
86		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
87		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
88		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
89		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
90	}
91	
92	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
93		mode := r.Mode
94		if mode == "" {
95			if r.Once {
96				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
97			} else {
98				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
99			}
100		}
101		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
102		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
103			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
104		}
105		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
106			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
107		}
108		return mode, idleInterval
109	}
110	
111	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
112	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
113	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
114	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
115	
116	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
117	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
118	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
119	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
120	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
121	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
122	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
123	
124	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
125	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
126	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
127	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
128	// advance the base branch.
129	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
130	
131	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
132	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
133	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
134	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
135	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
136	// worker.
137	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
138	
139	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
140	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
141	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
142	// triage:needs-investigation park.
143	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
144	
145	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
146	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
147	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
148	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
149	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
150	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are

518	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
519	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets

111		ref := candidateIterationRef(attemptID, cycleIndex)

113		if err != nil {

174	
175	// ShouldRetainCandidateRef returns true when the attempt outcome requires
176	// keeping the candidate ref for operator inspection and recovery.
177	// Only a successfully landed (merged) candidate allows the temporary ref to be
178	// cleaned up; preserved, conflicted, parked, and manual candidates retain it.
179	func ShouldRetainCandidateRef(status string) bool {
180		return status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
181	}
182	
183	// AttemptCycleResult is the outcome of AttemptCycleCoordinator.Run.
184	type AttemptCycleResult struct {
185		// Report is the final execute-bead report after all cycle phases complete.
186		Report ExecuteBeadReport
187		// Landed is true when the candidate was merged to the main branch.
188		Landed bool
189	}
190	
191	// AttemptCycleCoordinator owns the full live-worktree lifecycle for a single
192	// implementation attempt: implementation → optional checks → optional review →
193	// optional repair → land. It ensures terminal disposition is reached before
194	// the coordinator exits, regardless of which phase terminates the cycle.
195	//
196	// Pass and Lander are required. Nil optional fields (Checks, Reviewer, Repair,
197	// RefStore) skip their respective phases.
198	//
199	// Repair loops are reserved for future implementation. When Reviewer is non-nil
200	// the coordinator runs one pre-land candidate review before landing.
201	type AttemptCycleCoordinator struct {
202		Pass        ImplementationPass
203		Checks      CandidateCheckRunner // nil → skip pre-land checks
204		Reviewer    CandidateReviewer    // nil → skip candidate review (future)
205		Repair      RepairPass           // nil → skip repair loops (future)
206		Lander      CandidateLander
207		RefStore    CandidateRefStore // nil → no ref pinning
208		ProjectRoot string            // required when RefStore non-nil
209		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender // nil → no candidate-cycle event emission
210		// RepairMaxCycles caps append-only repair attempts after review_fixable_gap.
211		// Values <=0 default to one repair cycle.
212		RepairMaxCycles int
213	}
214	
215	// Run executes one attempt cycle: implementation → checks → land. Non-success
216	// statuses (no_changes, execution_failed, etc.) return without calling the
217	// lander.
218	// The caller is responsible for worktree cleanup after Run returns.
219	//
220	// When RefStore and ProjectRoot are set, Run pins a candidate ref under
221	// refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before checks and review.
222	// After a successful land the temporary ref is cleaned up; for preserved,
223	// conflicted, parked, or otherwise non-landed outcomes the ref is retained so
224	// operators can inspect and recover the candidate.
225	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) Run(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (AttemptCycleResult, error) {
226		candidate, err := c.Pass.Execute(ctx, beadID)
227		if err != nil {
228			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, err
229		}
230	
231		if candidate.Report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
232			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, nil
233		}
234	
235		repairCycles := 0
236		var pinnedRefs []string
237		for {
238			c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
239				Active:       true,
240				Phase:        "candidate",
241				CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
242				CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
243			})
244			c.pinCandidateRef(beadID, &candidate)
245			pinnedRefs = appendUniqueString(pinnedRefs, candidate.Report.CandidateRef)
246			c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
247				Active:       true,
248				Phase:        "candidate_pinned",
249				CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
250				CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
251				CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
252			})
253	
254			if c.Checks != nil {
255				c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
256					Active:       true,
257					Phase:        "checks",
258					CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
259					CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
260					CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
261				})
262				checksResult, checksErr := c.Checks.RunChecks(ctx, c.ProjectRoot, candidate)
263				if checksErr != nil {
264					checksResult.Detail = checksErr.Error()
265				}
266				if checksErr != nil || !checksResult.Passed {
267					report := candidate.Report
268					if checksErr != nil {
269						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
270					} else {
271						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
272					}
273					report.OutcomeReason = candidateChecksFailedEventKind
274					report.Detail = candidateChecksFailedDetail(checksResult.Detail)
275					if checksErr != nil && report.Error == "" {
276						report.Error = checksErr.Error()
277					}
278					c.appendCandidateChecksFailedEvent(beadID, report, checksResult)
279					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
280				}
281			}
282	
283			if c.Reviewer == nil {
284				break
285			}
286	
287			c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
288				Active:       true,
289				Phase:        "review",
290				CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
291				CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
292				CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
293				ReviewActive: true,
294			})
295			reviewResult, reviewErr := c.Reviewer.Review(ctx, c.ProjectRoot, candidate)
296			if reviewErr != nil {
297				report := candidate.Report
298				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
299				report.Detail = "pre-land review: " + reviewErr.Error()
300				report.ReviewVerdict = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict)
301				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
302				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, ReviewFindingClassMalfunction, reviewErr.Error())
303				return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
304			}
305	
306			verdict := Verdict(strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict))
307			switch verdict {
308			case VerdictApprove:
309				candidate.Report.ReviewVerdict = string(VerdictApprove)
310				candidate.Report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
311				break
312			case VerdictRequestChanges, VerdictBlock:
313				classification := c.classifyCandidateReview(reviewResult)
314				report := candidate.Report
315				report.ReviewVerdict = string(verdict)
316				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
317				if verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
318					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
319					report.Detail = "pre-land review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
320				} else {
321					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
322					report.Detail = "pre-land review: BLOCK"
323				}
324				if verdict == VerdictBlock && classification.Class == ReviewFindingClassMalfunction {
325					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
326					report.Detail = "pre-land review: " + ReviewFindingClassMalfunction
327				}
328				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, classification.Class, classification.Reason)
329				if !classification.AutomatedRepairEligible || c.Repair == nil {
330					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
331				}
332				if repairCycles >= c.maxRepairCycles() {
333					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
334					report.OutcomeReason = ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
335					report.Detail = "pre-land repair: " + ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
336					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
337				}
338				prompt := BuildRepairPrompt(repairPromptInput(beadID, candidate, reviewResult, repairCycles+1))
339				c.appendRepairCycleStartedEvent(beadID, candidate, repairCycles+1, classification.Class)
340				c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
341					Active:       true,
342					Phase:        "repair",
343					CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
344					CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
345					CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
346					RepairActive: true,
347				})
348				repaired, repairErr := c.Repair.Repair(ctx, candidate, prompt)
349				if repairErr != nil {
350					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
351					report.Detail = "pre-land repair: " + repairErr.Error()
352					report.Error = repairErr.Error()
353					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
354				}
355				repairCycles++
356				candidate = normalizeRepairedCandidate(candidate, repaired)
357				if candidate.Report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
358					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, nil
359				}
360				continue
361			default:
362				report := candidate.Report
363				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
364				report.Detail = "pre-land review: malformed verdict"
365				report.ReviewVerdict = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict)
366				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
367				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, ReviewFindingClassMalfunction, "malformed review verdict")
368				return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
369			}
370			break
371		}
372	
373		landed, err := c.Lander.Land(ctx, candidate)
374		if err != nil {
375			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, err
376		}
377	
378		// Propagate candidate metadata to the landed report when the lander does
379		// not set them (e.g. in tests that return candidate.Report directly).
380		if landed.CandidateRef == "" && candidate.Report.CandidateRef != "" {
381			landed.CandidateRef = candidate.Report.CandidateRef
382			landed.CycleIndex = candidate.Report.CycleIndex
383		}
384	
385		result := AttemptCycleResult{Report: landed, Landed: true}
386	
387		// Clean up the candidate ref after successful landing. For any non-success
388		// outcome (preserved, conflicted, parked, manual) the ref is retained so
389		// operators can inspect the candidate commit after the worktree is removed.
390		if c.RefStore != nil && c.ProjectRoot != "" && landed.CandidateRef != "" {
391			if !ShouldRetainCandidateRef(landed.Status) {
392				for _, ref := range pinnedRefs {
393					_ = c.RefStore.UnpinCandidateRef(c.ProjectRoot, ref)
394				}
395			}
396		}
397	
398		return result, nil
399	}
400	
401	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) recordCandidateCycleState(candidate CandidateResult, state CandidateCycleState) {
402		if candidate.WorktreePath == "" {
403			return
404		}
405		_ = WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState(c.ProjectRoot, candidate.WorktreePath, candidate.Report.AttemptID, state)
406	}
407	
408	func candidateChecksFailedDetail(detail string) string {
409		detail = strings.TrimSpace(detail)
410		if detail == "" {
411			return candidateChecksFailedEventKind
412		}
413		return candidateChecksFailedEventKind + ": " + detail
414	}
415	
416	func appendUniqueString(items []string, item string) []string {
417		item = strings.TrimSpace(item)
418		if item == "" {
419			return items
420		}
421		for _, existing := range items {
422			if existing == item {
423				return items
424			}
425		}
426		return append(items, item)
427	}
428	
429	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) pinCandidateRef(beadID string, candidate *CandidateResult) {
430		if candidate == nil || c.RefStore == nil || c.ProjectRoot == "" {
431			return
432		}
433		if candidate.Report.CandidateRef != "" {
434			return
435		}
436		ref, pinErr := c.RefStore.PinCandidateRef(
437			c.ProjectRoot,
438			candidate.Report.AttemptID,
439			candidate.CycleIndex,
440			candidate.Report.ResultRev,
441		)
442		if pinErr != nil {
443			return
444		}
445		candidate.Report.CandidateRef = ref
446		candidate.Report.CycleIndex = candidate.CycleIndex
447		if c.BeadEvents == nil {
448			return
449		}
450		body, _ := json.Marshal(CandidateCycleEventBody{
451			CandidateRef: ref,
452			CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
453			AttemptID:    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
454			BaseRev:      candidate.Report.BaseRev,
455			ResultRev:    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
456		})
457		_ = c.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
458			Kind: "candidate_cycle_pinned",
459			Body: string(body),
460		})
461	}
462	
463	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) appendCandidateChecksFailedEvent(beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, checksResult CandidateCheckResult) {
464		if c.BeadEvents == nil {
465			return
466		}
467		body, _ := json.Marshal(CandidateChecksFailedEventBody{
468			CandidateRef: report.CandidateRef,
469			CycleIndex:   report.CycleIndex,
470			AttemptID:    report.AttemptID,
471			BaseRev:      report.BaseRev,
472			ResultRev:    report.ResultRev,
473			Detail:       strings.TrimSpace(checksResult.Detail),
474			Artifacts:    append([]string(nil), checksResult.Artifacts...),
475		})
476		_ = c.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
477			Kind:    candidateChecksFailedEventKind,
478			Summary: report.Detail,
479			Body:    string(body),
480		})
481	}
482	
483	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) maxRepairCycles() int {
484		if c.RepairMaxCycles <= 0 {
485			return 1
486		}
487		return c.RepairMaxCycles
488	}
489	
490	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) classifyCandidateReview(review CandidateReviewResult) ReviewFindingClassification {
491		if review.Classification != "" {
492			return reviewClassification(review.Classification, nil, "", review.Classification == ReviewFindingClassFixableGap)
493		}
494		return ClassifyReviewFindings(&ReviewResult{
495			Verdict:   Verdict(strings.TrimSpace(review.Verdict)),
496			Rationale: strings.TrimSpace(review.Rationale),
497			PerAC:     append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
498			Findings:  append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
499		})
500	}

502	func repairPromptInput(beadID string, candidate CandidateResult, review CandidateReviewResult, repairCycleIndex int) RepairPromptInput {
503		return RepairPromptInput{
504			BeadID:               beadID,
505			BaseRev:              candidate.Report.BaseRev,
506			FailedCandidateRev:   candidate.Report.ResultRev,
507			CycleIndex:           repairCycleIndex,
508			ReviewRationale:      strings.TrimSpace(review.Rationale),
509			PerAC:                append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
510			Findings:             append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
511			VerificationCommands: append([]string(nil), review.VerificationCommands...),
512		}
513	}
514	
515	func normalizeRepairedCandidate(previous, repaired CandidateResult) CandidateResult {
516		if repaired.WorktreePath == "" {
517			repaired.WorktreePath = previous.WorktreePath
518		}
519		if repaired.CycleIndex <= previous.CycleIndex {
520			repaired.CycleIndex = previous.CycleIndex + 1
521		}
522		if repaired.Report.BeadID == "" {
523			repaired.Report.BeadID = previous.Report.BeadID
524		}
525		if repaired.Report.AttemptID == "" {
526			repaired.Report.AttemptID = previous.Report.AttemptID
527		}
528		if repaired.Report.BaseRev == "" {
529			repaired.Report.BaseRev = previous.Report.BaseRev
530		}
531		if repaired.Report.Status == "" {
532			repaired.Report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
533		}
534		if repaired.Report.CycleIndex == 0 && repaired.CycleIndex != 0 {
535			repaired.Report.CycleIndex = repaired.CycleIndex
536		}
537		repaired.Report.CandidateRef = ""
538		repaired.Report.ReviewVerdict = ""
539		repaired.Report.ReviewRationale = ""
540		return repaired
541	}

582	// WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState updates cleanup metadata and, when a
583	// matching run-state exists, the per-attempt run-state snapshot for a live
584	// candidate-cycle worktree.
585	func WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState(projectRoot, wtPath, attemptID string, state CandidateCycleState) error {
586		meta, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath)
587		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
588			return fmt.Errorf("write candidate cycle state: read metadata: %w", err)
589		}
590		meta.WorktreePath = firstNonEmpty(meta.WorktreePath, wtPath)
591		meta.ProjectRoot = firstNonEmpty(meta.ProjectRoot, projectRoot)
592		meta.AttemptID = firstNonEmpty(meta.AttemptID, attemptID)
593		applyCandidateCycleStateToMetadata(&meta, state)
594		if err := WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, meta); err != nil {
595			return err
596		}
597		if projectRoot == "" || meta.AttemptID == "" {
598			return nil
599		}
600		states, err := ReadRunStates(projectRoot)
601		if err != nil {
602			return nil
603		}
604		for i := range states {
605			current := states[i]
606			if current.AttemptID != meta.AttemptID {
607				continue
608			}
609			applyCandidateCycleStateToRunState(&current, state)
610			return WriteRunState(projectRoot, current)
611		}
612		return nil
613	}
614	
615	// MarkWorktreeActiveCycle sets the ActiveCandidateCycle flag in the worktree's
616	// cleanup metadata. The cleanup manager preserves any worktree with this flag
617	// set so it is never deleted mid-cycle before the coordinator disposes it.
618	func MarkWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath string) error {
619		return WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState("", wtPath, "", CandidateCycleState{
620			Active: true,
621			Phase:  "active",
622		})
623	}
624	
625	// ClearWorktreeActiveCycle clears the ActiveCandidateCycle flag in the
626	// worktree's cleanup metadata after the coordinator reaches terminal disposition.
627	func ClearWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath string) error {
628		meta, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath)
629		if err != nil {
630			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
631				return nil
632			}
633			return fmt.Errorf("clear active cycle: read metadata: %w", err)
634		}
635		meta.ActiveCandidateCycle = false
636		meta.CandidateCyclePhase = ""
637		meta.ReviewActive = false
638		meta.RepairActive = false
639		return WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, meta)
640	}
641	
642	func applyCandidateCycleStateToMetadata(meta *ExecutionCleanupMetadata, state CandidateCycleState) {
643		meta.ActiveCandidateCycle = state.Active
644		meta.CandidateCyclePhase = state.Phase
645		meta.CandidateRef = state.CandidateRef
646		meta.CandidateRev = state.CandidateRev
647		meta.CycleIndex = state.CycleIndex
648		meta.ReviewActive = state.ReviewActive
649		meta.RepairActive = state.RepairActive
650	}
651	
652	func applyCandidateCycleStateToRunState(runState *RunState, state CandidateCycleState) {
653		runState.CandidateCyclePhase = state.Phase
654		runState.CandidateRef = state.CandidateRef
655		runState.CandidateRev = state.CandidateRev
656		runState.CycleIndex = state.CycleIndex
657		runState.ReviewActive = state.ReviewActive
658		runState.RepairActive = state.RepairActive
659	}

1	package agent

11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"

18	// triageDecisionEventKind is the event kind used to record the triage
19	// policy's chosen action after a non-APPROVE review verdict.
20	const triageDecisionEventKind = "triage-decision"
21	
22	// PostMergeReviewInput bundles the data RunPostMergeReview needs from its
23	// caller — historically the execute-bead loop body, now the per-attempt
24	// pipeline owned by try.Attempt (ddx-a921ff01 C3). The fields mirror what
25	// the inlined block read from the worker, the candidate bead, the report,
26	// and the loop runtime.
27	type PostMergeReviewInput struct {
28		Bead          bead.Bead
29		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
30		Reviewer      BeadReviewer
31		Store         ExecuteBeadLoopStore
32		ProjectRoot   string
33		Rcfg          config.ResolvedConfig
34		NoReview      bool
35		Log           io.Writer
36		Assignee      string
37		Now           func() time.Time
38		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
39	}
40	
41	// PostMergeReviewOutput carries the review state machine's decision back to
42	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
43	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
44	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
45	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
46	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.
47	//
48	// RepairContext is non-nil when the BLOCK verdict was classified as a fixable
49	// gap and exactly one repair cycle has been scheduled for this result_rev.
50	// The caller should run a repair attempt with MinPower above
51	// RepairContext.ImplementerActualPower while preserving all explicit
52	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
53	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
54		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
55		Approved      bool
56		StoreErrOp    string
57		StoreErr      error
58		RepairContext *RepairContextFromReviewGroup
59	}
60	
61	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
62	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
63	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
64	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
65	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
66	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
67	// review events without reopening the bead.
68	//
69	// The review:skip label only skips when it carries a sibling
70	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
71	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
72	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
73	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
74	// still consulted without reopening the bead.
75	//
76	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
77	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
78	// reviewer transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp +
79	// StoreErr so callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern
80	// unchanged.
81	//
82	// This is the C3 (ddx-a921ff01) extraction of the 180-line review block
83	// previously inline in execute_bead_loop.go; the loop now sees a structured
84	// outcome and conflict-recovery / decomposition / no_changes paths remain
85	// in the loop body until subsequent C-series beads (C4, C5) move them too.
86	func RunPostMergeReview(ctx context.Context, in PostMergeReviewInput) PostMergeReviewOutput {
87		report := in.Report
88		out := PostMergeReviewOutput{Report: report, Approved: true}
89	
90		now := in.Now
91		if now == nil {
92			now = time.Now
93		}
94	
95		reviewSkipped := in.Reviewer == nil || in.NoReview
96		if !reviewSkipped && HasBeadLabel(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip") {
97			reviewSkipped = HasBeadLabelPrefix(in.Bead.Labels, "review:skip-reason:")
98		}
99	
100		if reviewSkipped {
101			if err := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
102				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
103				out.StoreErr = err
104				return out
105			}
106			out.Report = report
107			return out
108		}
109	
110		// AC 1: Pre-dispatch cost cap check — enforce max_billable_usd before each
111		// adversarial reviewer invocation. If the drain-level budget is already
112		// exhausted, classify as review-error: cost_cap_exceeded and feed the
113		// review_max_retries / review-manual-required retry path so the bead is
114		// left open and not silently closed.
115		if capTracker := in.ReviewCostCap; capTracker != nil {
116			if detail, capped := capTracker.Tripped(); capped {
117				class := evidence.OutcomeReviewCostCapExceeded
118				prior := CountPriorReviewErrors(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev)
119				attemptCount := prior + 1
120				maxRetries := in.Rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
121				if maxRetries <= 0 {
122					maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
123				}
124				body := ReviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, detail)
125				if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
126					_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
127						Kind:      "review-manual-required",
128						Summary:   class,
129						Body:      body,
130						Actor:     in.Assignee,
131						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
132						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
133					})
134					applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(
135						in.Store,
136						in.Bead.ID,
137						in.Assignee,
138						now().UTC(),
139						"review cost cap exhausted: "+class,
140						"review cost cap requires operator decision",
141						"raise max_billable_usd or reset the session budget before retrying",
142					)
143				} else {
144					_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
145						Kind:      "review-error",
146						Summary:   class,
147						Body:      body,
148						Actor:     in.Assignee,
149						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
150						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
151					})
152				}
153				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
154				report.Detail = "pre-close review: " + class
155				out.Report = report
156				out.Approved = false
157				return out
158			}
159		}
160	
161		implRouting := ImplementerRouting{
162			Harness:     report.Harness,
163			Provider:    report.Provider,
164			Model:       report.Model,
165			ActualPower: report.ActualPower,
166			Correlation: map[string]string{
167				"bead_id":    in.Bead.ID,
168				"attempt_id": report.AttemptID,
169				"session_id": report.SessionID,
170				"result_rev": report.ResultRev,
171			},
172		}
173		reviewGroup, reviewErr := runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx, in.Reviewer, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, implRouting)
174		reviewRes, reviewGroupErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(reviewGroup)
175		chargeReviewCost := func() bool {
176			budgetExceeded := false
177			if capTracker := in.ReviewCostCap; capTracker != nil && reviewGroup != nil {
178				deferred := false
179				for _, slot := range reviewGroup.Slots {
180					if slot.Result == nil {
181						continue
182					}
183					capTracker.Add(slot.Result.ReviewerHarness, slot.Result.CostUSD)
184					if detail, capped := capTracker.Tripped(); capped && !deferred {
185						_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
186							Kind:      "review-cost-deferred",
187							Summary:   "review-cost-deferred",
188							Body:      ReviewCostDeferredEventBody(report.ResultRev, slot.Result.CostUSD, capTracker.Spent(), capTracker.MaxUSD),
189							Actor:     in.Assignee,
190							Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
191							CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
192						})
193						if in.Log != nil {
194							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(in.Log, "review cost cap deferred (%s %s): %s\n", in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, detail)
195						}
196						deferred = true
197						budgetExceeded = true
198					}
199				}
200			}
201			return budgetExceeded
202		}
203		if reviewGroupErr != nil {
204			reviewErr = reviewGroupErr
205		}
206		if reviewErr != nil {
207			class := ClassifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
208			reviewerIndex, slotScoped := reviewErrorReviewerIndex(reviewGroup, reviewRes)
209			prior := CountPriorReviewErrors(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev)
210			if slotScoped {
211				prior = CountPriorReviewErrorsForSlot(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, reviewerIndex)
212			}
213			attemptCount := prior + 1
214			maxRetries := in.Rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
215			if maxRetries <= 0 {
216				maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
217			}
218			errSummary := EventBodySummary{
219				InputBytes:  0,
220				OutputBytes: 0,
221			}
222			if reviewRes != nil {
223				errSummary = EventBodySummary{
224					Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
225					Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
226					InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
227					OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
228					ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
229				}
230			}
231			if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
232				body := ReviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error())
233				if slotScoped {
234					body = ReviewErrorEventBodyForSlot(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewerIndex, reviewErr.Error())
235				}
236				body = AppendEventSummary(body, errSummary)
237				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
238					Kind:      "review-manual-required",
239					Summary:   class,
240					Body:      body,
241					Actor:     in.Assignee,
242					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
243					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
244				})
245				applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(
246					in.Store,
247					in.Bead.ID,
248					in.Assignee,
249					now().UTC(),
250					"review retry budget exhausted: "+class,
251					"review error retry budget requires operator decision",
252					"review the pre-close review failure and accept, retry, block, or cancel",
253				)
254			} else {
255				body := ReviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error())
256				if slotScoped {
257					body = ReviewErrorEventBodyForSlot(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewerIndex, reviewErr.Error())
258				}
259				body = AppendEventSummary(body, errSummary)
260				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
261					Kind:      "review-error",
262					Summary:   class,
263					Body:      body,
264					Actor:     in.Assignee,
265					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
266					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
267				})
268			}
269			if reviewRes != nil {
270				report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
271				report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
272			}
273			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
274			report.Detail = "pre-close review: " + class
275			out.Report = report
276			out.Approved = false
277			chargeReviewCost()
278			return out
279		}
280	
281		report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
282		report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
283		artifactPath, artifactErr := PersistReviewerStream(in.ProjectRoot, in.Bead.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
284		if artifactErr != nil && in.Log != nil {
285			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(in.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
286		}
287	
288		reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
289			Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
290			Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
291			InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
292			OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
293			ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
294		}
295		reviewBudgetExceeded := chargeReviewCost()
296		switch reviewRes.Verdict {
297		case VerdictApprove:
298			// Approved: record the verdict event and then close. The close must
299			// land AFTER the review event so the gate sees the terminal verdict.
300			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
301				Kind:      "review",
302				Summary:   "APPROVE",
303				Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
304				Actor:     in.Assignee,
305				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
306				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
307			})
308			if reviewBudgetExceeded {
309				// Reviewer cost tripped the budget cap; leave bead open so the
310				// loop's BudgetStop can stop the drain before the next claim.
311				out.Report = report
312				out.Approved = false
313				return out
314			}
315			if cerr := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
316				out.Report = report
317				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
318				out.StoreErr = cerr
319				return out
320			}
321		case VerdictRequestChanges:
322			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
323				Kind:      "review",
324				Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
325				Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
326				Actor:     in.Assignee,
327				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
328				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
329			})
330			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
331			report.Detail = "pre-close review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
332			out.Approved = false
333		case VerdictBlock:
334			rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
335			classification := ClassifyReviewFindings(reviewRes)
336			if rationale == "" && len(classification.Evidence) == 0 {
337				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
338					Kind:      "review-malfunction",
339					Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
340					Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
341					Actor:     in.Assignee,
342					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
343					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
344				})
345				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
346				report.Detail = "pre-close review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
347				report.ReviewRationale = ""
348				out.Report = report
349				out.Approved = false
350				return out
351			}
352			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
353				Kind:      "review",
354				Summary:   "BLOCK",
355				Body:      AppendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
356				Actor:     in.Assignee,
357				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
358				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
359			})
360			if classification.Class == ReviewFindingClassMalfunction {
361				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
362					Kind:      "review-malfunction",
363					Summary:   ReviewFindingClassMalfunction,
364					Body:      AppendEventSummary(classification.Reason, reviewSummary),
365					Actor:     in.Assignee,
366					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
367					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
368				})
369				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
370				report.Detail = "pre-close review: " + ReviewFindingClassMalfunction
371				out.Report = report
372				out.Approved = false
373				return out
374			}
375			if terminalClass := classifyTerminalReviewBlock(reviewRes); terminalClass != "" {
376				applyTerminalReviewBlock(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), terminalClass, report.ResultRev)
377				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock
378				report.Detail = "pre-close review: terminal " + terminalClass
379				out.Report = report
380				out.Approved = false
381				return out
382			}
383			// Non-terminal BLOCK: schedule one bounded repair cycle (review_fixable_gap).
384			// If a repair cycle has already been scheduled for this result_rev, fall
385			// through to the regular BLOCK triage path so the policy cannot loop.
386			if classification.AutomatedRepairEligible && !hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev) {
387				groupID := ""
388				if reviewGroup != nil {
389					groupID = reviewGroup.Bundle.GroupID
390				}
391				repairCtx := scheduleReviewFixableGapRepair(
392					in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(),
393					groupID, report.ResultRev,
394					strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale),
395					report.ActualPower,
396				)
397				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap
398				report.Detail = "pre-close review: review_fixable_gap"
399				out.Report = report
400				out.Approved = false
401				out.RepairContext = repairCtx
402				return out
403			}
404			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
405			report.Detail = "pre-close review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
406			out.Approved = false
407		}
408		if reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictBlock || reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
409			_ = applyReviewTriageDecision(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), report.Tier)
410		}
411	
412		out.Report = report
413		return out
414	}
415	
416	func runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, reviewer BeadReviewer, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error) {
417		if reviewer == nil {
418			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: reviewer required")
419		}
420		if groupReviewer, ok := reviewer.(reviewGroupReviewer); ok {
421			return groupReviewer.ReviewGroup(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
422		}
423		result, err := reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
424		group := &ReviewGroupResult{
425			BeadID:    beadID,
426			ResultRev: resultRev,
427			Slots: []ReviewGroupSlotResult{
428				{
429					ReviewerIndex: 0,
430					Result:        result,
431				},
432			},
433		}
434		if err != nil {
435			group.Slots[0].Error = err.Error()
436		}
437		return group, err
438	}
439	
440	func reviewErrorReviewerIndex(group *ReviewGroupResult, res *ReviewResult) (int, bool) {
441		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) < 2 {
442			return 0, false
443		}
444		if res != nil {
445			for _, slot := range group.Slots {
446				if slot.Result == res {
447					return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
448				}
449			}
450			return res.ReviewerIndex, true
451		}
452		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
453			if slot.Error != "" {
454				return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
455			}
456		}
457		return 0, true
458	}
459	
460	func reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group *ReviewGroupResult) (*ReviewResult, error) {
461		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) == 0 {
462			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: no review slots")
463		}
464		var firstApprove *ReviewResult
465		var firstNonApprove *ReviewResult
466		var firstResult *ReviewResult
467		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
468			if slot.Result != nil && firstResult == nil {
469				firstResult = slot.Result
470			}
471			if slot.Error != "" && slot.Result == nil {
472				if firstResult != nil {
473					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
474				}
475				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
476			}
477			if slot.Result == nil {
478				if firstResult != nil {
479					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
480				}
481				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
482			}
483			res := slot.Result
484			switch res.Verdict {
485			case VerdictApprove:
486				if !reviewResultHasACEvidence(res) {
487					return res, fmt.Errorf("%w: approve verdict without per-AC evidence", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
488				}
489				if firstApprove == nil {
490					firstApprove = res
491				}
492			case VerdictRequestChanges, VerdictBlock:
493				if firstNonApprove == nil {
494					firstNonApprove = res
495				}
496			default:
497				if firstNonApprove == nil {
498					firstNonApprove = res
499				}
500			}
501		}
502		if firstNonApprove != nil {
503			return firstNonApprove, nil
504		}
505		return firstApprove, nil
506	}
507	
508	func reviewResultHasACEvidence(res *ReviewResult) bool {
509		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictApprove {
510			return false
511		}
512		if len(res.PerAC) == 0 {
513			return false
514		}
515		for _, ac := range res.PerAC {
516			if strings.TrimSpace(ac.Evidence) == "" {
517				return false
518			}
519		}
520		return true
521	}
522	
523	// applyReviewTriageDecision runs the triage policy after a BLOCK or
524	// REQUEST_CHANGES review verdict has been recorded for `beadID`. It reads
525	// prior review events to compute the BLOCK count, biases the result toward
526	// re-attempt when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer
527	// (kind:review-pairing-degraded), then applies the chosen action by writing
528	// metadata, labels, and a kind:triage-decision event.
529	//
530	// Errors from the underlying store are best-effort: callers continue
531	// regardless so a triage-side failure cannot strand a bead in an
532	// inconsistent review state. This callout previously lived as a method on
533	// ExecuteBeadWorker; the C3
534	// extraction (ddx-a921ff01) moves it inside the post-merge-review pipeline
535	// driven by RunPostMergeReview / try.Attempt.
536	func applyReviewTriageDecision(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, currentTier string) error {
537		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
538		if err != nil {
539			return err
540		}
541	
542		var blockTimestamps []time.Time
543		var pairingDegraded []time.Time
544		for _, ev := range events {
545			switch {
546			case ev.Kind == "review" && strings.TrimSpace(ev.Summary) == "BLOCK":
547				blockTimestamps = append(blockTimestamps, ev.CreatedAt)
548			case ev.Kind == ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind:
549				pairingDegraded = append(pairingDegraded, ev.CreatedAt)
550			}
551		}
552	
553		// History excludes the current attempt. The just-appended BLOCK (if any)
554		// is the last entry in blockTimestamps and must not be counted as prior
555		// history per triage.Decide()'s contract.
556		historyCount := len(blockTimestamps)
557		if historyCount > 0 {
558			historyCount--
559		}
560		history := make([]triage.FailureMode, 0, historyCount)
561		for i := 0; i < historyCount; i++ {
562			history = append(history, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock)
563		}
564	
565		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
566		action := policy.Decide(beadID, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock, history)
567	
568		pairedDegraded := latestBlockPairedDegraded(blockTimestamps, pairingDegraded)
569		if pairedDegraded && action == triage.ActionEscalateTier {
570			action = triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext
571		}
572	
573		return applyTriageAction(store, beadID, actor, now, action, currentTier, pairedDegraded)
574	}
575	
576	// latestBlockPairedDegraded reports whether the most recent BLOCK event was
577	// paired with a review-pairing-degraded event from the same attempt window
578	// (between the previous BLOCK and this one).
579	func latestBlockPairedDegraded(blocks, pairing []time.Time) bool {
580		if len(blocks) == 0 || len(pairing) == 0 {
581			return false
582		}
583		latest := blocks[len(blocks)-1]
584		var prev time.Time
585		if len(blocks) >= 2 {
586			prev = blocks[len(blocks)-2]
587		}
588		for _, t := range pairing {
589			if t.After(prev) && !t.After(latest) {
590				return true
591			}
592		}
593		return false
594	}
595	
596	// applyTriageAction performs the side effects for a chosen Action: writes a
597	// tier-pin hint into bead.Extra (escalate_tier), moves the bead to proposed
598	// for operator-required outcomes, and always records a triage-decision event.
599	func applyTriageAction(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, action triage.Action, currentTier string, pairedDegraded bool) error {
600		body := map[string]any{
601			"action": string(action),
602			"mode":   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock),
603		}
604		if pairedDegraded {
605			body["pairing_degraded"] = true
606		}
607	
608		switch action {
609		case triage.ActionEscalateTier:
610			nextTier := nextEscalatedTier(currentTier)
611			body["tier_hint"] = string(nextTier)
612			_ = store.Update(beadID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
613				if b.Extra == nil {
614					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
615				}
616				b.Extra[TriageTierHintKey] = string(nextTier)
617			})
618		case triage.ActionOperatorRequired:
619			if err := store.ParkToProposed(beadID, bead.ParkPostReviewMalfunction, func(b *bead.Bead) {
620				if b.Extra == nil {
621					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
622				}
623				delete(b.Extra, TriageTierHintKey)
624				// Migration-only cleanup: defensive removal for legacy rows that escaped
625				// the lifecycle migration or arrived via external import.
626				b.Labels = removeBeadLabels(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, bead.LabelNeedsInvestigation)
627				clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b)
628				bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
629					Reason:          "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung",
630					Since:           now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
631					Source:          "ddx agent execute-loop",
632					SuggestedAction: "review the blocked attempt and accept, split, block, or cancel",
633					Summary:         "review BLOCK triage requires operator decision",
634				})
635			}); err != nil {
636				return err
637			}
638		}
639	
640		bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(body)
641		return store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
642			Kind:      triageDecisionEventKind,
643			Summary:   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock) + ": " + string(action),
644			Body:      string(bodyJSON),
645			Actor:     actor,
646			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
647			CreatedAt: now,
648		})
649	}
650	
651	func clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b *bead.Bead) {
652		b.Owner = ""
653		if b.Extra == nil {
654			return
655		}
656		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-at")
657		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-pid")
658		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-machine")
659		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-session")
660		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-worktree")
661		delete(b.Extra, "execute-loop-heartbeat-at")
662	}
663	
664	// nextEscalatedTier returns the next tier above `current`. An unrecognised or
665	// empty current tier defaults to standard; smart is its own ceiling.
666	func nextEscalatedTier(current string) escalation.ModelTier {
667		switch escalation.ModelTier(current) {
668		case escalation.TierCheap:
669			return escalation.TierStandard
670		case escalation.TierStandard:
671			return escalation.TierSmart
672		case escalation.TierSmart:
673			return escalation.TierSmart
674		default:
675			return escalation.TierStandard
676		}
677	}

data.text: Now let me look at the escalation test to understand its structure and the alignment test:

data.output:
1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"testing"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
11	)
12	
13	// --- Local mirrors of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus* so these tests do not import
14	// the agent package. Their string values must stay in sync with
15	// cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go; the agent package's
16	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guards against drift for the
17	// statuses that drive escalation.
18	const (
19		statusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
20		statusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
21		statusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
22		statusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
23		statusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
24		statusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
25		statusSuccess                    = "success"
26	)
27	
28	// --- EscalationSummary ---
29	
30	// recordingAppender captures events appended by AppendEscalationSummaryEvent
31	// so tests can assert on the kind, summary, and JSON body.
32	type recordingAppender struct {
33		events []struct {
34			id    string
35			event bead.BeadEvent
36		}
37	}
38	
39	func (r *recordingAppender) AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error {
40		r.events = append(r.events, struct {
41			id    string
42			event bead.BeadEvent
43		}{id: id, event: event})
44		return nil
45	}
46	
47	// TestEscalationSummary exercises the 3-tier escalation scenario from the
48	// bead: cheap fails, standard fails, smart succeeds. The emitted
49	// kind:escalation-summary event body must contain all three tier records
50	// with correct statuses, costs, and a winning_tier/wasted_cost_usd roll-up.
51	func TestEscalationSummary(t *testing.T) {
52		attempts := []TierAttemptRecord{
53			{Tier: "cheap", Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: statusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.02, DurationMS: 1200},
54			{Tier: "standard", Harness: "codex", Model: "standard-model", Status: statusNoChanges, CostUSD: 0.15, DurationMS: 3400},
55			{Tier: "smart", Harness: "claude", Model: "smart-model", Status: statusSuccess, CostUSD: 0.80, DurationMS: 9000},
56		}
57	
58		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, "smart")
59		require.Equal(t, "smart", summary.WinningTier)
60		require.Len(t, summary.TiersAttempted, 3)
61		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, summary.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
62		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, summary.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9, "cheap (0.02) + standard (0.15) wasted, smart succeeded")
63		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", summary.TiersAttempted[0].Tier)
64		assert.Equal(t, statusExecutionFailed, summary.TiersAttempted[0].Status)
65		assert.Equal(t, statusNoChanges, summary.TiersAttempted[1].Status)
66		assert.Equal(t, statusSuccess, summary.TiersAttempted[2].Status)
67	
68		// Now wire through AppendEscalationSummaryEvent and verify the emitted
69		// event has kind:escalation-summary, a summary line, and a JSON body
70		// that round-trips to the same EscalationSummary.
71		appender := &recordingAppender{}
72		err := AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender, "ddx-test-1", "test-actor", attempts, "smart", time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
73		require.NoError(t, err)
74		require.Len(t, appender.events, 1)
75	
76		ev := appender.events[0]
77		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-test-1", ev.id)
78		assert.Equal(t, "escalation-summary", ev.event.Kind)
79		assert.Equal(t, "test-actor", ev.event.Actor)
80		assert.Contains(t, ev.event.Summary, "winning_tier=smart")
81		assert.Contains(t, ev.event.Summary, "attempts=3")
82	
83		var decoded EscalationSummary
84		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.event.Body), &decoded))
85		assert.Equal(t, "smart", decoded.WinningTier)
86		require.Len(t, decoded.TiersAttempted, 3)
87		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Tier)
88		assert.Equal(t, "agent", decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Harness)
89		assert.Equal(t, "cheap-model", decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Model)
90		assert.Equal(t, statusExecutionFailed, decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Status)
91		assert.InDelta(t, 0.02, decoded.TiersAttempted[0].CostUSD, 1e-9)
92		assert.Equal(t, int64(1200), decoded.TiersAttempted[0].DurationMS)
93		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, decoded.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
94		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, decoded.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9)
